questions.q: The Lok Sabha is also known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha is the House of the People, the lower house of Parliament.

questions.q: How are members of the Lok Sabha chosen?
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha members are directly elected by the people through general elections.

questions.q: What is the normal term of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha has a normal term of five years from the date of its first sitting.

questions.q: Within Parliament, the Lok Sabha is referred to as the:
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha is the lower house of the bicameral Parliament of India.

questions.q: Who presides over the sittings of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Speaker is the presiding officer of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Who was the first Speaker of independent India's Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: G.V. Mavalankar served as the first Speaker of the Lok Sabha, from 1952.

questions.q: The maximum strength of the Lok Sabha mentioned in the Constitution is:
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides a maximum strength of 552 members for the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to which House?
questions.explanation: Under the Constitution, the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: A motion expressing loss of majority support for the government is moved in the Lok Sabha as a:
questions.explanation: A no-confidence motion tests whether the Council of Ministers retains the confidence of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: An electoral division that elects one Lok Sabha member is called a:
questions.explanation: Each Lok Sabha member is elected from a territorial constituency.

questions.q: What is the minimum age to be eligible for election to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 25 years of age to be eligible for membership of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha is elected by:
options: The members of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Speaker is chosen by the members of the Lok Sabha from among themselves.

questions.q: Who decides whether a bill is a money bill?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
The Chairman of the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: Under Article 110, the Speaker's decision on whether a bill is a money bill is final.

questions.q: The composition of the Lok Sabha and the allocation of seats to states is dealt with in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 81 deals with the composition of the Lok Sabha and the allocation of seats; the normal five-year term is set out separately in Article 83.

questions.q: During a national Emergency, the term of the Lok Sabha may be extended by Parliament by a period not exceeding:
questions.explanation: While a Proclamation of Emergency is in operation, the Lok Sabha's life may be extended by up to one year at a time.

questions.q: Who can dissolve the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Under Article 85, the President may dissolve the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: When the Speaker's office is vacant or the Speaker is absent, who presides over the Lok Sabha?
options: The leader of the largest party
questions.explanation: The Deputy Speaker presides in the absence of the Speaker.

questions.q: In a general election, voters of the Lok Sabha cast their vote based on which principle?
options: Proportional representation by single transferable vote
questions.explanation: Lok Sabha members are elected by direct election on the basis of universal adult franchise.

questions.q: Certain seats in the Lok Sabha are reserved for which groups?
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides for reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes in the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The maximum strength of 552 for the Lok Sabha includes members from the states and:
questions.explanation: The maximum strength comprises members representing the states and the union territories.

questions.q: To whom does the Speaker of the Lok Sabha tender a resignation?
questions.explanation: The Speaker resigns by writing addressed to the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The total elected strength of the Lok Sabha for general elections, excluding nominated members, is generally:
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha currently has 543 elected seats filled through direct election from constituencies.

questions.q: The provision allowing reservation of seats in the Lok Sabha for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes has been periodically extended through which mechanism?
questions.explanation: Reservation of seats for these communities has been extended over time through constitutional amendments.

questions.q: A Speaker of the Lok Sabha may be removed from office by a resolution passed by:
options: A simple majority of all then members
questions.explanation: The Speaker may be removed by a resolution passed by a majority of all the then members of the Lok Sabha, after 14 days notice.

questions.q: Once a money bill is passed by the Lok Sabha and sent to the Rajya Sabha, the Rajya Sabha must return it within:
questions.explanation: The Rajya Sabha must return a money bill within fourteen days; otherwise it is deemed passed.

questions.q: The provision that allowed nomination of members of the Anglo-Indian community to the Lok Sabha lapsed after which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 104th Amendment, effective in 2020, ended the provision for nominated Anglo-Indian members.

questions.q: The allocation of Lok Sabha seats among states is based on which constitutional principle?
options: Population, as far as practicable uniform across states
questions.explanation: Seats are allotted so that the ratio of seats to population is, as far as practicable, the same for all states.

questions.q: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha exercises a casting vote in the case of:
questions.explanation: The Speaker does not vote in the first instance but exercises a casting vote in the case of a tie.

questions.q: A Lok Sabha member may resign their seat by writing addressed to the:
questions.explanation: A member resigns by writing addressed to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The Speaker of the outgoing Lok Sabha vacates office when?
options: Just before the first meeting of the new Lok Sabha
On the new Speaker being sworn in by the President
questions.explanation: The Speaker does not vacate office on dissolution but continues until immediately before the first meeting of the newly constituted Lok Sabha.
